description: Machine-readable manifest splitting the Label 309 conformance corpus into vectors that MUST be byte-identical across every conforming implementation versus vectors where only structural (semantic) parity is required. Any pair of conforming implementations (e.g. TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go, native mobile) MUST produce byte-identical output for every entry under `byte_identical`. The `structural_parity_only` entries assert the same outcome (recovered plaintext, accepted/rejected verdict, emitted error code) but are not byte-pinned, because their inputs are non-deterministic (random keypairs/nonces) or their wire form admits writer-dependent variation (JSON whitespace and key ordering).

rules.byte_identical: For these vectors `encode(decode(bytes)) == bytes` and every emitted output byte string MUST match across implementations. A diverging byte is a conformance failure.
rules.structural_parity_only: For these vectors implementations MUST agree on the semantic result (round-trip success, tamper/verdict outcome, emitted error code) but the exact byte encoding is NOT pinned.

byte_identical.vector_class: Canonical CBOR primitives (RFC 8949)
byte_identical.notes: Bytewise canonical encoding, externally pinned to RFC 8949. Round-trip identity encode(decode(bytes)) == bytes.

byte_identical.vector_class: Canonical CBOR — decode rejection (duplicate-key, malformed)
byte_identical.notes: Includes the duplicate-key rejection vector; every implementation MUST reject the same byte sequences with the same outcome.

byte_identical.vector_class: Content hash — SHA-256 / BLAKE2b-256 + dual-hash equivalence
HKDF-SHA-256 (RFC 5869 A.1/A.2/A.3)
byte_identical.notes: Externally pinned, transcribed verbatim from RFC 5869.

byte_identical.vector_class: HKDF-SHA-256 zero-length salt (RFC 5869 section 2.2 absent salt)
byte_identical.notes: Pins the absent-salt extract (salt is the empty octet string, substituted with HashLen zero bytes) against a non-empty info string, mirroring the slots_mac HKDF call so the construction's zero-length salt is byte-exact rather than library-dependent.

byte_identical.vector_class: Argon2id v1.3 KDF + fixed parameter constants
byte_identical.notes: Byte-identical within Argon2id implementation tolerance (RFC 9106).

byte_identical.vector_class: Passphrase normalization profile (cardano-poe-pw-norm-v1) pinned through Argon2id to the CEK
byte_identical.notes: Each positive case pins the normalized UTF-8 bytes of the pinned Unicode-16.0 NFKC + White_Space-collapse profile AND the resulting 32-byte CEK under a fixed Argon2id salt and the floor parameters; error cases pin ENC_PASSPHRASE_EMPTY, ENC_PASSPHRASE_UNNORMALIZABLE, and the raw 4096-byte input cap, including their precedence.

byte_identical.vector_class: Pinned Unicode 16.0.0 NFKC normalization oracle
byte_identical.notes: Derived from the UCD 16.0.0 NormalizationTest corpus (c1 -> NFKC pairs plus assigned/unassigned codepoint samples); every implementation's pinned normalizer replays it byte-identically. The passphrase profile builds on this oracle; the end-to-end profile-to-CEK pins live in kdf/passphrase-normalization.json.

byte_identical.vector_class: XChaCha20-Poly1305 (draft-irtf-cfrg-xchacha-03) + ChaCha20-Poly1305 (RFC 8439)
byte_identical.notes: Externally pinned AEAD ciphertext + tag.

byte_identical.vector_class: X25519 (RFC 7748) + low-order-point validation
ML-KEM-768 + X25519 (X-Wing) — keygen / encaps / decaps / SHAKE expansion
byte_identical.notes: X-Wing primitive output emitted byte-exact by the reference implementation; 1120-byte ciphertext and 1216-byte public key reproduced from pinned inputs. The deterministic-draft10 file pins the full seed -> pk -> (eseed -> ct, ss) chain copied verbatim from draft-connolly-cfrg-xwing-kem-10 Appendix C.

byte_identical.vector_class: Ed25519 signing/verification (RFC 8032 §7.1 Test 2 signer)
Strict-Ed25519 differentiating fixture (torsion / cofactored-vs-strict)
byte_identical.notes: Sourced from the c2sp/CCTV corpus. Every conforming verifier MUST emit valid:false byte-exact. A verifier returning true is using a non-conformant (cofactored) primitive.

byte_identical.vector_class: COSE_Sign1 — Sig_structure / protected header / build / strict-Ed25519 verify
byte_identical.notes: RFC 9052 encoder mechanics plus the production-form record-level COSE_Sign1 (detached payload, domain-separated to_sign prefix). to-sign-verbatim pins the protected-header byte-preservation rule: Sig_structure[1] takes the published COSE_Sign1[0] bytes verbatim, never a re-canonicalisation. attached-detached-transform pins the CIP-30 payload detachment: one signature verifies in both payload carriages, and only the detached form may be embedded in sigs[i].

byte_identical.vector_class: Wallet COSE_Sign1 (CIP-30) — valid per-wallet shape-vectors
byte_identical.notes: Synthetic deterministic shape-vectors for the per-wallet CIP-30 signData wire form (inline cose_key + protected-header address); verifiers MUST accept the inline cose_key path byte-exact.

byte_identical.vector_class: Label-309 carriage — chunk-array transport + auxiliary-data envelope forms
byte_identical.notes: Whole-body chunk-array reassembly (boundaries are semantics-free; zero-length chunks tolerated), the carriage-error taxonomy (CHUNK_TOO_LARGE / MALFORMED_CBOR), and the three Conway auxiliary-data envelope forms with type-and-tag-only dispatch (an untagged map is always the metadata map). Fixed input bytes; accepted forms pin the exact reassembled body.

byte_identical.vector_class: Cardano binding — tx-hash / auxiliary_data_hash integrity + confirmation depth
byte_identical.notes: blake2b-256 bindings over synthetic minimal transaction bytes (TX_INTEGRITY_MISMATCH / METADATA_NOT_FOUND dispositions) and the depth = tip − block + 1 arithmetic with the threshold comparison pinned on both sides.

byte_identical.vector_class: Seed-derive — seed -> Ed25519 + X25519 + X-Wing keys (3 seeds)
byte_identical.notes: HKDF info strings cardano-poe-ed25519-v1 / cardano-poe-x25519-v1 / cardano-poe-mlkem768x25519-v1, L=32. Ed25519/X25519 byte-pinned; X-Wing seed + 1216-byte public key emitted byte-exact by the reference implementation.

byte_identical.vector_class: Seed-derive — recipient bech32 encodings (age X25519 + age1pqc X-Wing)
byte_identical.notes: Sealed-PoE recipient public-key encodings; bech32 strings MUST match byte-exact. recipients-from-seed keys the encodings by identity seed; recipient-strings-kat keys them by raw public key (public key <-> exact string, both directions) and pins the HRP / visible-prefix distinction: HRP 'age' renders 'age1...', HRP 'age1pqc' renders 'age1pqc1...'.

byte_identical.vector_class: Seed-derive — checksummed identity-seed encoding (l309-seed bech32 + raw hex)
byte_identical.notes: The 32-byte identity seed <-> 'l309-seed-' bech32 string codec (BIP-173 classic, no length limit). Encode MUST emit the pinned UPPERCASE 'L309-SEED-1...' display form byte-exact; parse MUST accept both single-case bech32 forms and 64-hex-digit raw seeds (0x prefix and whitespace tolerated) and MUST reject each negative input with the pinned construction-API error code.

byte_identical.vector_class: Multi-recipient sealed-PoE wrap — x25519 KEM
byte_identical.notes: Per-slot epk + wrap, slots_mac, and the segmented-STREAM ciphertext for pinned inputs (the item's hashes map is a construction input). Per-slot KEK = HKDF(x25519_shared, salt=SHA-256(cardano-poe-x25519-kek-salt-v1 || enc.nonce || epk || pub_R), info=cardano-poe-kek-v1); the wrap AEAD AAD is the cardano-poe-kek-v1 label literal. slots_mac = HMAC-SHA-256(HKDF(CEK, info=cardano-poe-slots-mac-v1), slots_hash) where slots_hash = SHA-256(cardano-poe-slots-transcript-v1 || canonicalEncode(SLOTS_TRANSCRIPT)) and SLOTS_TRANSCRIPT binds scheme, path, aead, kem, nonce, the canonical slot set, and hashes_hash = SHA-256(cardano-poe-item-hashes-v1 || canonicalEncode(item.hashes)). Content is encrypted under payload_key = HKDF(CEK, salt=enc.nonce, info=cardano-poe-payload-v1) in 65536-byte STREAM chunks with empty per-chunk AAD.

byte_identical.vector_class: Multi-recipient sealed-PoE wrap — mlkem768x25519 (X-Wing) KEM
byte_identical.notes: Per-slot kem_ct (a single 1120-byte byte string) + wrap, slots_mac, and the segmented-STREAM ciphertext. The hybrid KEK is salt-bound: KEK = HKDF(xwing_shared, salt=SHA-256(cardano-poe-xwing-kek-salt-v1 || enc.nonce || kem_ct || pub_R), info=cardano-poe-kek-mlkem768x25519-v1), so the wrap binds the envelope nonce, the slot's own X-Wing ciphertext, and the recipient public key; the wrap AEAD AAD is the cardano-poe-kek-mlkem768x25519-v1 label literal. slots_mac, hashes_hash, and the STREAM content layer follow the same transcript-bound construction as the classical path.

byte_identical.vector_class: Multi-recipient sealed-PoE unwrap — round-trip + multi-private-key matching
byte_identical.notes: Recovered plaintext = original; slot/private-key matching is deterministic given pinned inputs. Per-slot acceptance folds the KEM validity bit, the wrap-open, and the slots_mac check over slots_hash into one constant-time decision; the hybrid path adds the KEM_CT_LENGTH_MISMATCH pre-decapsulation guard. unwrap-shadow-slot pins the forged-slot-before-honest-slot positive: a fabricated slot ahead of the honest one fails the per-slot fold and the envelope still decrypts under the honest CEK. unwrap-duplicate-recipient pins the legitimate same-recipient-in-two-slots case (fresh distinct ephemerals, same CEK): both slots recover the same CEK, so the CEK-conflict defence does NOT trip and the record decrypts — a verifier must keep decrypting honest recipient padding and reject only when two matching slots recover DIFFERENT CEKs.

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE segmented STREAM content layer
byte_identical.notes: Pins the chunk framing under a fixed payload_key: per-chunk nonce = uint88_be(counter) || final_flag, empty AAD, 65536-byte plaintext chunks with 16-byte tags; non-final chunks carry exactly 65536 plaintext bytes and a zero-length final chunk occurs only for empty plaintext. Positives cover empty plaintext, one byte, an exact single chunk, and a payload crossing the chunk boundary. Negatives apply an ordered transform list to a positive's ciphertext and MUST fail as ciphertext tampering: a flipped tag in the final chunk and in a non-final chunk, truncation that removes the final chunk, truncation inside the final chunk (a tail too short to be a chunk), trailing data after the final chunk, a short non-final chunk, a blob below the single-tag floor, and a correctly authenticated zero-length final chunk forged after a non-final chunk (an empty final chunk encodes only the empty plaintext, so it MUST be rejected even on tag success). Ciphertext bytes MUST be byte-identical across implementations.

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE passphrase path — Argon2id KAT + negatives
byte_identical.notes: CEK = Argon2id(normalize(passphrase), salt, params) under the cardano-poe-pw-norm-v1 normalization profile (pinned Unicode 16.0 NFKC; a scheme-fixed constant, never on the wire); params are pinned at the producer floor (m=65536, t=3, p=4). The published blob is commitment(32) || STREAM chunks: the commitment is keyed by an HKDF leaf of the CEK over the passphrase transcript hash (SHA-256(cardano-poe-passphrase-transcript-v1 || canonicalEncode(PASSPHRASE_TRANSCRIPT)), binding salt, params, nonce, and hashes_hash) and is verified in constant time before any chunk opens; payload_key = HKDF(CEK, salt=enc.nonce, info=cardano-poe-payload-passphrase-v1). Blob and commitment bytes MUST be byte-identical across implementations. The negatives pin one indistinguishable generic failure for wrong passphrase, tampered salt/params, a flipped commitment header, a spliced hashes claim, and a blob below the 48-byte structural floor; the typed envelope-shape raises (unregistered aead or passphrase alg, a non-24-byte nonce, a salt outside 16..64 bytes, Argon2id params below the registry floors) fire before any KDF work; and the typed input rejections ENC_PASSPHRASE_EMPTY (whitespace-only) and ENC_PASSPHRASE_UNNORMALIZABLE (unassigned codepoint) precede the blob-length floor.

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE canonical transcript bytes
byte_identical.notes: Pins the exact canonicalEncode output of SLOTS_TRANSCRIPT (x25519 + mlkem768x25519) and PASSPHRASE_TRANSCRIPT, plus the hashes_hash pin for single- and dual-entry item.hashes maps, derived from the committed wrap-n3, wrap-hybrid-n1, and passphrase-n1 inputs. These byte strings are the messages hashed into slots_hash / the passphrase transcript hash, so pinning them catches a cross-SDK canonical-encoding mismatch directly rather than only via a downstream slots_mac / commitment failure. The STREAM content layer uses empty per-chunk AAD, so no AAD object bytes exist to pin. Every emitted hex byte MUST match across implementations.

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE KEK salt derivation (both KEMs)
byte_identical.notes: Pins kek_salt = SHA-256(cardano-poe-x25519-kek-salt-v1 || enc.nonce || epk || pub_R) for the classical path and kek_salt = SHA-256(cardano-poe-xwing-kek-salt-v1 || enc.nonce || kem_ct || pub_R) for the hybrid path (single 1120-byte X-Wing ciphertext, 1216-byte recipient public key), plus the resulting KEK. Both salts MUST be byte-identical across implementations.

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE unwrap negatives (tamper detection + input validation)
byte_identical.notes: The matched-false vectors pin the failure taxonomy: nothing opened (wrong recipient on both KEMs, a swapped nonce — which changes every per-slot KEK salt — and a garbage kem_ct of valid length, which decapsulates implicitly to a pseudorandom shared secret with no distinct decaps error) → WRONG_RECIPIENT_KEY; a wrap that opens under a tampered slots_mac, a spliced hashes claim, or a sibling slot's flipped kem_ct (slots_mac covers every slot's KEM material) → TAMPERED_HEADER; a stream failure → TAMPERED_CIPHERTEXT. The raise vectors pin the typed pre-trial input rejections (unsupported scheme/aead/kem, empty slots, wire-length mismatches including both sides of the exact 1120-byte hybrid kem_ct check, duplicate per-slot KEM material → ENC_SLOTS_DUPLICATE_KEM_MATERIAL, and the key-input shape errors), all validated before any KEM or AEAD primitive runs.

byte_identical.vector_class: Sealed-PoE construction negatives (header binding / all-zero shared / cross-path / invalid recipient key)
byte_identical.notes: All-zero X25519 shared secret (the slot is treated as failed; no CEK recovered → WRONG_RECIPIENT_KEY), cross-path confusion (a slots-shaped record decrypted with a passphrase input, or vice versa, MUST be refused before any AEAD), header-binding flips of scheme/aead/kem with shape-valid slots (the transcript-bound acceptance fails), hybrid header-binding via nonce swap, duplicate hybrid kem_ct (KEK reuse), and an X-Wing recipient public key of correct length whose ML-KEM ek fails the FIPS 203 modulus check (the sealing API rejects before any slot is produced → INVALID_RECIPIENT_KEY). The rejection verdict is pinned; envelope bytes are deterministic.

byte_identical.vector_class: Record canonical CBOR — maximal + mixed-signature positive records
byte_identical.notes: The maximal record exercises the full v1 wire surface (items with single-text-string URIs, merkle, a shape-valid sealed envelope, a single-byte-string sigs[0].cose_sign1, supersedes, tolerated extension keys); encode(decode(bytes)) == bytes. The mixed-signature record pins a path-1 (in-signature kid) entry and a path-2 (CIP-30 cose_key + address) entry in one signature list, in both orderings.

byte_identical.vector_class: Merkle (rfc9162-sha256) — root + inclusion-proof + leaves-list KATs
byte_identical.notes: Fixed-input positive vectors for tree sizes 1,2,3,4,5,7 (RFC 9162 §2.1.1 root construction, §2.1.3.2 inclusion-proof verification). Roots, per-leaf sibling proofs, and the canonical-CBOR leaves-list encodings (with and without the optional leaf_alg key) MUST be byte-identical across implementations. encode(decode(cbor)) == cbor for every leaves-list.

byte_identical.vector_class: Merkle (rfc9162-sha256) — leaves-list rejection
byte_identical.notes: Companion leaves-list schema violations; every implementation MUST reject the same byte sequences with the same emitted error code.

byte_identical.vector_class: Structural validator — negative / positive / role-dependent / resource-bound corpora
byte_identical.notes: Each case pins cbor_hex input and the exact expected code sets the structural validator MUST emit: expected_error_codes (error severity), expected_info_codes (info severity), or expected_by_role (the dual-severity public vs recipient/strict dispositions of the unknown-envelope rule). validator_options, where present, names the assumed configuration (supportedCriticalExtensions, deployment-pinned resource bounds). Every Part A error code in the registry is exercised by at least one fixture across these files. Single source of truth for cross-language validator parity.

structural_parity_only.vector_class: Cross-implementation interop — externally-published sealed record (classical + hybrid)
structural_parity_only.notes: A sealed record produced by an independent implementation through its public SDK surface only, then validated and trial-decrypted by a separate recipient verifier. The record bytes are canonical CBOR in the v1 wire shapes (single-byte-string kem_ct, plain text-string URIs, chacha20-poly1305-stream64k content format); the fixture's chain facts use the wire representations (block_time as integer POSIX seconds, confirmation_depth counted in blocks as tip − block + 1). Implementations MUST agree on the recovered CEK, the matched item/slot index, and the trial-decrypt verdict; the surrounding fixture envelope is not byte-pinned.
Decrypt(Encrypt(plaintext)) == plaintext and tampered-tag rejection. The fixed-input KAT variants in this same area carry the byte-pinned guarantee.
Round-trip agreement of the derived shared secret. Deterministic byte-equality is covered by the fixed-vector KATs in the same area.
Round-trip agreement of signature verification. Deterministic byte-equality is covered by the fixed-vector KATs in the same area.

structural_parity_only.vector_class: Wallet COSE_Sign1 (CIP-30) — rejection shape-vectors (tampered / missing / wrong-network)
structural_parity_only.notes: Synthetic shape-vector negatives: every verifier MUST reject these with the same verdict (tampered address binding / missing REQUIRED address / wrong network header byte, all WALLET_ADDRESS_MISMATCH). The rejection outcome is pinned; the underlying bytes vary per wallet.
Out-of-range seed lengths MUST yield INVALID_SEED_LENGTH across implementations.
